steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is “languages like Scheme” — a plural noun phrase with modifying prepositional and participial phrases.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content: Choice B uses “are not,” the only form that maintains grammatical agreement. A, C, and D all use singular verbs that violate subject-verb agreement. That matches choice B — B is correct.
